setting.note: Müller's Romania is the Banat under Ceaușescu, where four young ethnic-German friends leave a German-speaking farming village for the city to study, and the Securitate follows them into lecture halls, the student hostel, the factories they are posted to and the interrogation rooms. The map runs from the village on the Timiș plain to Timișoara's centre and university, then reaches east to the capital and out of the country to exile.

rights.originalWork: In copyright. Herta Müller, first published in German as Herztier, 1994. This book is mapped as a cited work: we store none of its text and reproduce no passages. The places are facts and the notes are our own.
rights.textSource: None stored. A cited book reproduces no text.
rights.territoryNotes: Locations and original commentary only. No reproduction of the copyrighted text, in any territory.
rights.attribution: The Land of Green Plums, Herta Müller (1994). Locations mapped by Read the Places; not affiliated with or endorsed by the author or publisher.

palette.note: The green of the unripe plums that give the book its title, held against the grey of the surveillance state. A cited book's colour is not a quotation, because we store no text to quote; it still has to be legible, and this clears WCAG AA on paper.
